Off Calendar Nov-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. B. DOUGLAS ROBBINS, Esq. of Tong Robbins LLP (Tel: 650-240-2402) for plaintiff;
Atty. LIZA C. MILANES, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for defendant 2100 Green Street, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BYERLY's Motion To Reopen Discovery.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

The motion to reopen discovery is granted to the extent hereinafter set forth.

The disclosure of the report of Ferrari Moe, including the destructive testing and related input from Fidelity Roofing, to a testimonial expert witness, constituted a waiver of attorney work product confidentiality under rules analogous to Evidence Code Section 912. The waiver could not have been discovered before non-expert discovery had closed and the testimonial expert's file was produced.

In view of the potential importance of this evidence and the apparent diligence and absence of fault on the part of Plaintiff Byerley, the Court exercises its discretion under CCP Sec. 2024.050 to order discovery reopened to permit discovery of Ferrari Moe, Fidelity Roofing and the San Francisco Building Department as to communications contained in or related to the consultant report of Ferrari Moe and excluding any other communications to counsel to Defendant 2100 Green Street for the purpose of assisting counsel in preparation for trial.

Counsel for Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to the pro tem judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com. The proposed order is to leave the discovery completion date blank.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BYERLEY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists whether the roof(s) at issues are "occupied roof(s)" within the meaning of the Building Code. Additionally, motion for summary adjudication is proper only if it disposes of an entire cause of action and such motion is framed by the pleadings. The fifth cause of action in the fifth amended complaint seeks money damages and punitive damages (paragraphs 91 and 92), the issue of damages is not disposed of by this motion. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
